prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `88207623`.
codex_response:
Implemented `88207623` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_88207623/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_88207623/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_88207623/generator.py#L142), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_88207623/notes.md). The rule is: each yellow `4` shape is mirrored across its red `2` vertical axis, and the mirrored copy is recolored with the opposite-side marker’s color while preserving the original axis, shape, and marker. No new shared primitive was needed.

I used the Opus hint as the starting hypothesis and discarded the Sonnet hint because it described wedge filling instead of exact reflection; that correction is recorded in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_88207623/notes.md#L1). The generator matches the task family by explicitly constructing red axes, irregular yellow side-shapes, and single marker cells chosen from the reflected copy, then cropping and mirroring into ARC-sized layouts. Verification passed on the official task (`3/3`), the build produced `1000` examples, and `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 88207623` passed `1000/1000`. I also visually checked [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/88207623/previews/originals.png) against [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/88207623/previews/preview_01.png), and the generated distribution looks on-family.
